title: קְדוּשְׁתָּאוֹת לְשַׁבָּת זָכוֹר | Qedushtaot for Shabbat Zakhor, by Elazar ben Qallir
author: Isaac Gantwerk Mayer (transcription & naqdanut)

coauthors.name: Isaac Gantwerk Mayer (transcription & naqdanut)
coauthors.bio: From a family of musicians, Isaac Gantwerk Mayer believes that creative art is one of the most powerful ways to get in touch with the divine. He composes music and poetry in Hebrew and English. (He also translates and authors his own original works.) Isaac runs a Jewish music transcription service, which will transcribe and set any Jewish music in any language, recorded or written. Contact his service on Facebook or via his music blog.

coauthors.name: Elazar ben Qallir
coauthors.bio: Elazar ben Qallir, also known as Eleazar Kalir, Killir, Qalir or El'azar HaKalir (c. 570 – c. 640) was a Hebrew poet whose classical liturgical verses, known as piyut, have continued to be sung through the centuries during significant religious services, including those on Tisha b'Av and on the sabbath after a wedding. He was one of Judaism's earliest and most prolific of the paytanim, Hebrew liturgical poets. He wrote piyutim for all the main Jewish festivals, for special Sabbaths, for weekdays of festive character, and for the fasts. Many of his hymns have found their way into festive prayers of the Ashkenazi Jews' nusaḥ. (via wikipedia).

excerpt: The complete qedushta cycle for Shabbat Zakhor by Qallir, including all the catenae of verses and repeated refrains often skipped in Ashkenazi practice. Alphabetical texts are translated with alphabetical acrostics.
content:
The complete qedushta cycle[1] colloquially called “yotsrot,” although it is not technically yotsrot but rather qerovot  for Shabbat Zakhor by Qallir, including all the catenae of verses and repeated refrains often skipped in Ashkenazi practice. Alphabetical texts are translated with alphabetical acrostics.

Note: “The CAUSE” is used to translate the Divine Name YHVH, based on the philosophical idea of God as the Prime Mover and on the interpretation of the Name as a causative form of the copula – “causes to be.” This translation also uses the plural pronouns They/Their/Them to refer to God as a pluralis majestatis, and to avoid the implications of God being assigned a gender. (Find Ibn Ezra’s commentary on Genesis 1:1 for a discussion of the pluralis majestatis when referring to God.) All divine referents (pronouns, epithets, names) are rendered in unicase.

Source (Hebrew) Translation (English)
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה
יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ
וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ׃
אֱלֹהֵי אַבְרָהָם אֱלֹהֵי יִצְחָק וֵאלֹהֵי יַעֲקֹב׃
הָאֵל
הַגָּדוֹל הַגִּבּוֹר וְהַנּוֹרָא
אֵל עֶלְיוֹן׃
גּוֹמֵל חֲסָדִים טוֹבִים
וְקוֹנֵה הַכֹּל
וְזוֹכֵר חַסְדֵי אָבוֹת
וּמֵבִיא גוֹאֵל לִבְנֵי בְנֵיהֶם
לְמַֽעַן שְׁמוֹ בְּאַהֲבָה׃
מֶֽלֶךְ
עוֹזֵר
וּמוֹשִֽׁיעַ
וּמָגֵן׃
Blessed are You,
o Cause, our God
and God of our ancestors
God of Abraham, God of Isaac, and God of Jacob.
The Deity,
great and mighty and awesome;
the almighty Deity,
who well repays kindness,
and possesses all,
and remembers the kindnesses of ancestors
and sends a redeemer to their descendants
for Their Name’s sake, in love.
Sovereign,
Help
and Rescue
and Shield —
רשות
Reshut
מִסּוֹד חֲכָמִים וּנְבוֹנִים
וּמִלֶּֽמֶד דַּֽעַת מְבִינִים
אֶפְתְּחָה פִּי בְּשִׁיר וּרְנָנִים
לְהוֹדוֹת וּלְהַלֵּל פְּנֵי שׁוֹכֵן מְעוֹנִים׃
By the counsel of sages and scholars,
and the study of knowledge-understanders,
let me open my mouth in song and cheer
to thank and praise the countenance of the Dweller on High.[2] This brief reshut was not written by Qallir. It is inserted into the Ashkenazi rite here to justify the practice of inserting piyyutim. 
מגן
Magen
אַזְכִּיר סֶֽלָה זִכְרוֹן מַעֲשִׂים
בַּיָּמִים הָאֵֽלֶּה נִזְכָּרִים וְנַעֲשִׂים
גָּחוֹן גָּח מִבֵּין עֲכָסִים
דֵּרְאוֹנוֹ לְהַזְכִּיר לְרֶֽקֶב כְּעָסִים׃
Always I will recall the remembrance of acts
By these days remembered and kept as facts.
Creeping out from among the snakes on its ribcage,
Disgusting to recall, rotting in rage.
הַחֽוֹחַ הֵנֵץ מִגַּלְגַּל וְדַרְדַּר
וּמִדּוֹר לְדוֹר גֻּלְגַּל וְדֻרְדַּר
זֵֽכֶר עֲוֺן אֲבוֹתָיו הׇקְדַּר
חַטַּאת אִמּוֹ בְּלִי לְהַעְדַּר׃
Erupting from tumbleweed and thistle, the thorn
From age to age would whirl on, unworn.
Gloomed he his father’s eyes by iniquity to recall.[3] A midrash found in multiple places, including Midrash Tanḥuma Ki Tetze 4:1, states that Isaac went blind from looking at Esau. 
He sinned against his mother; let it not fall!
טָרַף אַף וַיַּשְׁחֵת רַֽחַם
יְחוּמָתוֹ מִקְּלֹט בְּהֵרָיוֹן יַֽחַם
כְּהׇפְקַד לְשָׁלִישׁוֹ חֵטְא נֹֽחַם
לֻבַּט לְהִלָּחֵם בְּלוֹחֲמֵי לָֽחֶם׃
In rage he tore, destroying the womb;
Jerking his conceiver not to enjoy the bedroom.[4] Midrash Tanḥuma Ki Tetze 4:1 states that in being born, Esau injured Rebecca’s uterus, leaving her unable to orgasm. 
Kin third-generation[5] ’Amaleq, the grandson of Esau.  was appointed vengeance’s sin:
Let them hastily fight those who took the bread in.[6] Israel, that ate the manna. 
מַהֲלַךְ אַרְבַּע מֵאוֹת פַּרְסָה
נָע וְנָד מִכְמֹֽרֶת לְפׇרְסָה
סָע מִשֵּׂעִיר וְהִכְמִין פְּרוּסָה
עָיֵף וְיָגֵעַ מְבוּשָׁיו לְסׇרְסָה׃
Making the four-hundred parasang walk[7] Midrash Tanḥuma Beshallaḥ 25:2 states ‘Amaleq had to travel four hundred parasangs to reach Israel.  he tread,
Nomadically traveling with a net to spread.
On his way from Seir, he hid it laid out,
Planning, for shame! to mutilate the tired and worn out.
פּוֹעֵל שְׂטִימַת בְּכוֹרָה לַעֲכֹר
צְמִיתוּת צֹאן קׇדָשִׁים לִמְכֹּר
קְלוֹנוֹ הׇחֳרַט בָּעֵט לִזְכֹּר
רֵאשִׁית גּוֹיִם לְגַלַּע בְּזָכוֹר׃
Quite enraged at the disrupted birthright, he acted
Resolving to sell the holy flock for slavery protracted.
Stylus-engraved is his disgrace to remember
To be the first nation to be scratched with “Remember!”
שַׁדַּי זָכַר לַֽיְלָה חוֹלֵק
שֹֽׁרֶשׁ וְעָנָף בַּחֲלַקְלַק לְהַחֲלֵק
תְּמוּר כִּי חֲשָׁלָיו הִמְלֵק
תָּבַע לְהִזָּכֵר מַֽעַשׂ עֲמָלֵק׃
Upon remembering the night-divider,[8] Abraham, referencing the Covenant of the Pieces in Genesis 15  the Surveyor[9] A translation of the divine name Shaddai, based on Bereshit Rabbah 46:3 (on Genesis 17:1) that the name means “the One who said ‘enough’ (dai).” 
Vitiated in slipperiness the wood and root of the slayer.
Exchange for strangling those yonder delayed,
Zealously They demand we recall ‘Amaleq’s raid.
In most modern Ashkenazi practice, the catenae of verses inserted after the first three piyyutim are not recited.
כַּכָּתוּב׃
זָכ֕וֹר אֵ֛ת אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֥ה לְךָ֖ עֲמָלֵ֑ק
בַּדֶּ֖רֶךְ בְּצֵאתְכֶ֥ם מִמִּצְרָֽיִם׃ (דברים כה:יז)
As it is written:
Remember what ‘Amaleq did to you
on the way as you went out from Egypt. (Deuteronomy 25:17)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
וַיַּרְא֙ אֶת־עֲמָלֵ֔ק
וַיִּשָּׂ֥א מְשָׁל֖וֹ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר
רֵאשִׁ֤ית גּוֹיִם֙ עֲמָלֵ֔ק
וְאַחֲרִית֖וֹ עֲדֵ֥י אֹבֵֽד׃ (במדבר כד:כ)
And it is said:
And he saw ‘Amaleq
and raised his tune and said:
first of nations is ‘Amaleq,
but its end will be final destruction. (Numbers 24:20)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
זֵ֣כֶר צַ֭דִּיק לִבְרָכָ֑ה
וְשֵׁ֖ם רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִרְקָֽב׃ (משלי י:ז)
And it is said:
The memory of the righteous is for a blessing,
and the name of the wicked rots. (Proverbs 10:7)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
יִזָּכֵ֤ר ׀ עֲוֺ֣ן אֲ֭בֹתָיו אֶל־יְהֹוָ֑ה
וְחַטַּ֥את אִ֝מּ֗וֹ אַל־תִּמָּֽח׃ (תהלים קט:יד)
And it is said:
Let iniquity to his father be recalled to the Cause;
and do not erase the sin at his mother! (Psalms 109:14)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
וְהַיָּמִ֣ים הָ֠אֵ֠לֶּה נִזְכָּרִ֨ים וְנַעֲשִׂ֜ים בְּכׇל־דּ֣וֹר וָד֗וֹר
מִשְׁפָּחָה֙ וּמִשְׁפָּחָ֔ה
מְדִינָ֥ה וּמְדִינָ֖ה
וְעִ֣יר וָעִ֑יר
וִימֵ֞י הַפּוּרִ֣ים הָאֵ֗לֶּה לֹ֤א יַֽעַבְרוּ֙ מִתּ֣וֹךְ הַיְּהוּדִ֔ים
וְזִכְרָ֖ם לֹא־יָס֥וּף מִזַּרְעָֽם׃ (אסתר ט:כח)
And it is said:
And these days are recalled and done in each and every generation,
each and every family,
each and every state,
each and every city,
and these days of Purim will not pass from amongst the Jews,
and their memory will not end from their seed. (Esther 9:28)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
אֵ֥ין זִכְר֖וֹן לָרִאשֹׁנִ֑ים
וְגַ֨ם לָאַחֲרֹנִ֜ים שֶׁיִּהְי֗וּ
לֹֽא־יִהְיֶ֤ה לָהֶם֙ זִכָּר֔וֹן
עִ֥ם שֶׁיִּהְי֖וּ לָאַחֲרֹנָֽה׃ (קהלת א:יא)
And it is said:
There is no memory of the former,
and so too the latter that will be
will have no memory,
as they will be at the latter. (Ecclesiastes 1:11)
לָאַחֲרֹנָֽה יִסְעוּ שֶׁבָּם שָׁלַט
כִּי הֶעָנָן מַטָּם פָּלַט
לְעֵת יִמָּחֶה וְלֹא יֻמְלַט
יְגֹנֵן עַם גָּנוֹן וּמֻפְלָט׃
At the latter going out among them, he prevailed,
for the cloud excluded the tribe that trailed.[10] According to Midrash Tanḥuma Ki Tetze 10:1, the tribe of Dan, the hindmost tribe, was excluded from the cloud of glory on account of being idolators, and were thus the tribe that ‘Amaleq defeated. 
None will escape when they are erased;
They shield the people shielded and saved in haste.
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה מָגֵן אַבְרָהָם׃
Blessed are You, o Cause, shield of Abraham.
אַתָּה גִּבּוֹר לְעוֹלָם אֲדֹנָי
מְחַיֵּה מֵתִים אַתָּה רַב לְהוֹשִֽׁיעַ׃
מַשִּׁיב הָרֽוּחַ וּמוֹרִיד הַגָּֽשֶׁם׃
מְכַלְכֵּל חַיִּים בְּחֶֽסֶד
מְחַיֵּה מֵתִים בְּרַחֲמִים רַבִּים׃
סוֹמֵךְ נוֹפְלִים
וְרוֹפֵא חוֹלִים
וּמַתִּיר אֲסוּרִים
וּמְקַיֵּם אֱמוּנָתוֹ לִישֵׁנֵי עָפָר׃
מִי כָמֽוֹךָ בַּֽעַל גְּבוּרוֹת
וּמִי דוֹמֶה לָּךְ׃
מֶֽלֶךְ מֵמִית וּמְחַיֶּה
וּמַצְמִֽיחַ יְשׁוּעָה׃
וְנֶאֱמָן אַתָּה לְהַחֲיוֹת מֵתִים —
You are mighty forever, Master,
You bring life to the dead, greatly saving.
You turn the wind and send down the rain.
You provide for life in kindness,
reviving the dead in great mercy,
lift the fallen,
heal the sick,
free the bound,
and keep faith with sleepers in the dust.
Who is like You, Master of Powerful Acts,
and who can be compared to You?
Sovereign, who brings death and life
and causes salvation to sprout.
And trusted are You to revive the dead —
מחיה
Meḥayeh
תְּמִימִים בְּעוֹדָם בְּסִין רְפוּדִים
שִׁבּוּץ עֲדִי עֲדָיִים אֲפוּדִים
רָֽצוּ שְׁלוֹל מַתַּן לַפִּידִים
קָפְצוּ יָדַֽיִם רָפוֹת בִּרְפִידִים׃
Zenithal ones, yet in Sin,[11] Exodus 16:1  were enclothed
Yes, in finery wrapped, as befits the betrothed.
Exactly when taking the gift of flame
Weak at Rephidim, their hands became lame.[12] Midrash Tanḥuma, Beshallaḥ 25:2 states that ‘Amaleq had to travel through five nations’ land to reach Israel. 
צוֹרֵר אִסֵּף כׇּל־אֲסַפְסוּף
פּוֹשֵֽׁעַ סְבִיבוֹת מַחֲנָם אָסוּף
עֲלוֹת פְּעָמֵֽימוֹ מִמְּצוּלוֹת סוּף
סִבֵּר לְקׇמְלָם כְּקָנֶה וָסוּף׃
Villainously he horded up all his horde
Upon their camp to do crime as they gathered toward.
To those whose feet ascended from the deep-sea reeds,
Sought he to cut them down like canes and reeds.
נֻדַּד וַיָּֽעׇף שָׁלֹשׁ פְּעָמִים
מְחַזֵּר בְּנִינֵי חֲמִשָּׁה עֲמָמִים
לֵץ הֻכָּה וּפֶֽתִי הֶעֱמִים
כֻּלַּל לְהִתְיַחֵס נְדִיבֵי עַמִּים׃
Roaming, he flew at them on three occasions,[13] Exodus 17, Deuteronomy 25, and I Samuel 15, according to B. Megillah 7a. 
Quickly going by the descendants of five nations.[14] Midrash Tanḥuma, Beshallaḥ 25:4 states that ‘Amaleq could only attack because Israel’s hands had grown weak in the fulfillment of the mitzvot. 
Prudent was the simple when the mocker was struck,[15] Jethro. Midrash Tanḥuma, Yitro 3:2 states that Jethro had heard about the destruction of ‘Amaleq before converting. 
On this, to join the princely people he had the luck.
יָהַר לֵץ וְהֶעֱמִיד מְסִלָּה
טִיכּוּס בַּחוּרַי בְּזִמָּה סִלָּה
חַמּוּקֵי יְרֵכַי בָּם לְפׇסְלָה
זֶֽרַע לְהַרְבִּֽיעַ וּלְהַתְעִיב סְגֻלָּה׃
Notoriously, the mocker set up a highway
Mashing down my young men with behavior astray.
Looking to defile my rounded thighs,
Knocked up the treasure the way they despise.[16] Midrash Tanḥuma, Beshallaḥ 25:2 states that ‘Amaleq commited male-on-male rape against Israel. 
וַיִּשְׂטֹם שְׂטִימַת יְשִׁישׁוֹ הַמְּחֻבָּא
הֵחֵל לָבוֹא מֵרֹאשׁ בַּמַּחְבָּא
דָּגַר שֵׁנִית בְּחֶֽרֶב לְהוּבָה
גָּשׁ בַּשְּׁלִישִׁי בַּעֲלִיל בְּחוֹבָה׃
Jealous, like his grandfather’s grudge, he was hidden
Initially coming out from being hidden.
He broke out the second time with a flaming sword,
Going forth the third time, from our sin, aboveboard.
בְּמׇשְׁלוֹ שֶׁשְׁכִי בְּאוֹם מוֹשְׁלִים
בְּפֶֽרֶק עָמַד מְסוֹר נְשׁוּלִים
אָמְרוּ לְכוּ וְנַכְחִיד נִכְשָׁלִים
אָז כְּמֵאָז זִנֵּב כׇּל־הַנֶּחֱשָׁלִים׃
From the Sheshkhite’s[17] Nebuchadnezzar. Sheshakh is atbash cipher for Babylonia, a term used already in Jeremah 25:26.  rule over the ruling folk,
Encamped he to inform on refugees as he spoke.
Declaring, “Come, let us destroy those much maligned,”
By the same way he had anciently tailed those behind.
כַּכָּתוּב׃
אֲשֶׁ֨ר קָֽרְךָ֜ בַּדֶּ֗רֶךְ
וַיְזַנֵּ֤ב בְּךָ֙ כׇּל־הַנֶּחֱשָׁלִ֣ים אַֽחֲרֶ֔יךָ
וְאַתָּ֖ה עָיֵ֣ף וְיָגֵ֑עַ
וְלֹ֥א יָרֵ֖א אֱלֹהִֽים׃ (דברים כה:יח)
As it is written:
…who came upon you on the way
and tailed you, all those behind after you,
as you were tired and weary,
and had no fear of God. (Deuteronomy 25:18)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
אָמְר֗וּ לְ֭כוּ וְנַכְחִידֵ֣ם מִגּ֑וֹי
וְלֹֽא־יִזָּכֵ֖ר שֵֽׁם־יִשְׂרָאֵ֣ל עֽוֹד׃ (תהלים פג:ה)
And it is said:
They said, “Come, let us destroy them from nationhood,
and let the name of Israel no more be recalled.” (Psalms 83:5)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
לֵ֣ץ תַּ֭כֶּה
וּפֶ֣תִי יַעְרִ֑ם
וְהוֹכִ֥יחַ לְ֝נָב֗וֹן
יָבִ֥ין דָּֽעַת׃ (משלי יט:כה)
And it is said:
You strike the mocker
and the simple becomes prudent;
but reprove the intelligent
and he will understand knowledge (Proverbs 19:25)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
הֶן־כׇּל־אֵ֭לֶּה יִפְעַל־אֵ֑ל
פַּעֲמַ֖יִם שָׁל֣וֹשׁ עִם־גָּֽבֶר׃ (איוב לג:כט)
And it is said:
Hey, all these the Deity does,
twice, three times to a man. (Job 33:29)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
כָּרוּ־לִ֣י זֵדִ֣ים שִׁיח֑וֹת
אֲ֝שֶׁ֗ר לֹ֣א כְתוֹרָתֶֽךָ׃ (תהלים קט:פה)
And it is said:
The insolent dug pits for me,
which is not per Your Teaching. (Psalms 119:85)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
תָּשִׁ֨יב לָהֶ֥ם גְּמ֛וּל יְהֹוָ֖ה
כְּמַעֲשֵׂ֥ה יְדֵיהֶֽם׃ (איכה ג:סג)
And it is said:
Turn back to them recompense, Cause,
as per their handiwork. (Lamentations 3:64)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
תִּמְצָ֣א יָ֭דְךָ לְכׇל־אֹיְבֶ֑יךָ
יְ֝מִֽינְךָ֗ תִּמְצָ֥א שֹׂנְאֶֽיךָ׃ (תהלים כא:ט)
And it is said,
Your hand is enough for all Your enemies;
Your right hand is enough for Your foes. (Psalms 21:9)
שֹׂנְאֶֽיךָ הֵם יִלְבְּשׁוּ בֹֽשֶׁת
כְּמוֹ בְּצַלְעִי שָׂמְחוּ בַיַּבָּֽשֶׁת
בַּעֲטוֹתְךָ עֲשֶֽׂרֶת עֶדְיֵי תִלְבּֽוֹשֶׁת
לְהַגְשִׁים תֶּֽחִי לְצוֹלַֽעַת תַּחְבֹּֽשֶׁת׃
Your foes will wear their disgrace,
As they rejoiced at my fall out of thirst at that place.[18] Rephidim, where Israel had no water. 
When You are wrapped in Your ten garments of grace,[19] Find the midrash first found in Pesiqta d-Rav Kahana 22:5, but also found in Devarim Rabbah 2:37, which lists ten times Israel uses the language of “clothing” to praise God. 
Rain down life to treat the limping one’s face.
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה מְחַיֵּה הַמֵּתִים׃
Blessed are You, o Cause, who revives the dead.
משלש
Meshalesh
אֲצִילֵי מְרֵעִי נֶֽכֶד שֵׂעִיר
תּוֹלְדוֹת אֵלֶּה בְנֵי־שֵׂעִיר
בְּקַשָּׁם אָצוּ אִשִּׁי לְהַבְעִיר
שְׁאוֹנָם חָרְשׁוּ כְּשָׂדֶה עִיר׃
As my tormentors’ zealous foes, the heirs of Seir,
Brood of “yonder children of Seir,”
Charged with their straw, my fire to sear
Exultantly plowing my city clear.
גּוֹי גָּדוֹל שֶׁלֹּא הִזְכַּֽרְתָּ
רִשְׁיוֹנוֹתָיו לָמָה אָז זָכַֽרְתָּ
דְּגָלִים בְּלֹא הוֹן מָכַֽרְתָּ
קְדוֹשִׁים בַּזּוֹנָה וּבַיַּֽיִן הִמְכַּֽרְתָּ׃
Did You not recall the nation great?
Why, then, did You remember their sinful state?
Exchanging the bannered hosts for a price of no weight,
Vine’s blood and whores as the holy’s rebate!
הִזָּכֵר לָאֲדוֹמִים נָוֶה נִאֲרוּ
צָרִים אֲשֶׁר חוֹמוֹת עִרְעֲרוּ
וְעַד הַיְסוֹד עָֽרוּ עָֽרוּ
פִּימוֹ דִּבְּרוּ בְגֵאוּת וּפָעָרוּ׃
For the Edomites who burned the Sanctum, recall,
Unpleasant ones who battered the wall.
“Get it to the foundation, let it fall, let it fall!”
They gaped open their mouths and shouted in gall.
זֶה אֵין לְפָנֶֽיךָ שִׁכְחָה
עֶבְרָתָם שְׁמָֽרָה נֶֽצַח מִלְּשַׁכְּחָה
חֵֽלֶף לֹא־יָדְעוּ עֲשׂוֹת נְכוֹחָה
סִפְרָם חֲתוֹם לְיוֹם תּוֹכֵחָה
טַֽעַם חוֹק בְּדָת מוּכַח
נִזָּכֵר כִּי לֹא תִשָּׁכַח
יוֹם בֹּא עִמָּם לְהִתְוַכַּח
מִכַּאן צִוִּֽיתָ לְעַמְּךָ לֹא תִּשְׁכָּח׃
Hence, You, before whom none forget
Store their rage forever, not to forget
In that they did not know to do as they were let,
Ratify their book for the day they’ll regret!
Just as the law quotes, it demonstrates
Keenly it will be put in mind, lest the memory abates
Let Them come strive with them on that very date;
Marking Your people not to forget per Your mandate!
כַּכָּתוּב׃
וְהָיָ֡ה בְּהָנִ֣יחַ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֶ֣יךָ ׀
לְ֠ךָ֠ מִכׇּל־אֹ֨יְבֶ֜יךָ מִסָּבִ֗יב בָּאָ֙רֶץ֙
אֲשֶׁ֣ר יְהֹוָה־אֱ֠לֹהֶ֠יךָ נֹתֵ֨ן לְךָ֤ נַחֲלָה֙ לְרִשְׁתָּ֔הּ
תִּמְחֶה֙ אֶת־זֵ֣כֶר עֲמָלֵ֔ק מִתַּ֖חַת הַשָּׁמָ֑יִם
לֹ֖א תִּשְׁכָּֽח׃ (דברים כה:יט)
As it is written:
And when the Cause your God makes you rest
from all your enemies around in the land
that the Cause your God is giving you as an estate to inherit,
you will erase the memory of ‘Amaleq from under heaven;
do not forget! (Deuteronomy 25:19)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
זְכֹ֤ר יְהֹוָ֨ה ׀
לִבְנֵ֬י אֱד֗וֹם
אֵת֮ י֤וֹם יְֽר֫וּשָׁלָ֥͏ִם
הָ֭אֹ֣מְרִים עָ֤רוּ ׀ עָ֑רוּ
עַ֗֝ד הַיְס֥וֹד בָּֽהּ׃ (תהלים קלז:ז)
And it is said:
Remember, Cause,
the children of Edom,
on the day of Jerusalem,
saying “Raze it, raze it
down to its foundation!” (Psalms 137:7)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
תִּרְדֹּ֤ף בְּאַף֙ וְתַשְׁמִידֵ֔ם
מִתַּ֖חַת שְׁמֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃ (איכה ג:סו)
And it is said:
Tear after them in rage and destroy them
from under the Cause’s heavens. (Lamentations 3:66)
יָקַשׁ לֵץ לְחָרֵף צְבָאוֹת
הַחֲתוּמֵי בְרִית בְּצָבָא וָאוֹת
וְהֻפְקַד מִדָּה בְּמִדָּה לְתוֹצָאוֹת
דַּֽעַת כִּי לְעֵת הַמְּצִיאוֹת
הוּא יִפָּקֵד בְּשֵׁם יְהֹוָה צְבָאוֹת׃
Jeering, he set a wounding tripline
Upon the troops sealed with the covenant sign.
Deemed measure for measure, payback condign,
As knowledge that at the chosen deadline
He will be accounted for by the Cause divine.
כַּכָּתוּב׃
כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת
פָּקַ֕דְתִּי אֵ֛ת אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֥ה עֲמָלֵ֖ק לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל
אֲשֶׁר־שָׂ֥ם לוֹ֙ בַּדֶּ֔רֶךְ
בַּעֲלֹת֖וֹ מִמִּצְרָֽיִם׃ (שמואל א׳ טו:ב)
As it is written:
Thus says the Cause of Forces:
I have accounted for what ‘Amaleq did to Israel,
setting upon them on the path
as they went up from Egypt. (I Samuel 15:2)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
הֲלֽוֹא־שָׁמַ֤עְתָּ לְמֵֽרָחוֹק֙
אוֹתָ֣הּ עָשִׂ֔יתִי מִ֥ימֵי קֶ֖דֶם וִיצַרְתִּ֑יהָ
עַתָּ֣ה הֲבֵאתִ֔יהָ
וּתְהִ֗י לְהַשְׁא֛וֹת גַּלִּ֥ים
נִצִּ֖ים עָרִ֥ים בְּצֻרֽוֹת׃ (ישעיה לז:כו, מלכים ב׳ יט:כה)
And it is said:
Did you not hear from afar?
I did that, from days of yore I crafted it,
now I have brought it about,
and it has happened; to ruin
to desolate heaps fortified cities. (Isaiah 37:26, II Kings 19:25)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
מֵתִים֙ בַּל־יִ֣חְי֔וּ
רְפָאִ֖ים בַּל־יָקֻ֑מוּ
לָכֵ֤ן פָּקַ֙דְתָּ֙ וַתַּשְׁמִידֵ֔ם
וַתְּאַבֵּ֥ד כׇּל־זֵ֖כֶר לָֽמוֹ׃ (ישעיה כו:יד)
And it is said:
The dead shall not live,
the ghosts shall not rise,
thus You accounted for them and wiped them out,
ending all their memory. (Isaiah 26:14)
וְנֶאֱמַר׃
יִמְלֹ֤ךְ יְהֹוָ֨ה ׀ לְעוֹלָ֗ם
אֱלֹהַ֣יִךְ צִ֭יּוֹן לְדֹ֥ר וָדֹ֗ר
הַֽלְﬞלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ (תהלים קמו:י)
And it is said:
The Cause reigns forever;
your God, Zion, from generation to generation;
praise Yah! (Psalms 146:10)
וְאַתָּ֥ה קָד֑וֹשׁ י֝וֹשֵׁ֗ב תְּהִלּ֥וֹת יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ (תהלים כב:ד)
And You are holy, enthroned on Israel’s praise. (Psalms 22:4)
אל נא
El Na
אֵל נָא!
בְּלָשׁוֹן אֲשֶׁר הִזְכַּֽרְתָּ לְזוֹכְרֶֽיךָ זְכוֹר
בּוֹ בְלָשׁוֹן הִזְכִּירֽוּךָ נָא אַתָּה זְכוֹר
וְאִם־הֵמָּה כְּאָדָם עָבְרוּ בְרִית מִלִּזְכּוֹר
וְאַתָּה אֵל וְלֹא־אִישׁ לָמָּה לֹא־תִזְכּוֹר
Deity, please!
By the phrase that You made Your rememberers recall, “remember”
By that very phrase they make You recall – “please remember!”
And if they, mortals, breached the covenant they should remember,
You are a Deity, not a mortal, so why don’t You remember?
בְּזֹאת יָדַֽעְתִּי כִּי־יֵשׁ־לְךָ לִזְכּוֹר
אֲבָל תָּשֽׁוֹחַ עָלַי נַפְשִׁי עַד־זָכוֹר תִּזְכּוֹר
וּמַה־כֹּחִי כִּי־אֲיַחֵל לְקֵץ זָכוֹר
וּמַה־קִּצִּי כִּי־אַאֲרִיךְ נַפְשִׁי עַד־שֶׁתִּזְכּוֹר
By this I know that You have it in You to remember,
But my soul sinks in me until You remember, remember![20] Find Lamentations 3:20 
And what strength do I have to wait for the final “Remember?”
And what patience to draw out my soul until You remember?
אִם לֹא־לְמַעֲנִי תִּזְכּוֹר
לְמַעַנְךָ וּלְמַֽעַן יְרוּשָׁלַֽיִם זְכוֹר
חוֹק כִּי־לֹא־תִשָּׁכַח עֵדוּת זְכוֹר
וְאוֹמֵר אִם־אֶשְׁכָּחֵךְ עַתָּה לָהּ זְכוֹר
If not for my sake You will remember,
Then for Your own, and for Jerusalem’s, remember.
The law, the testimony not to be forgotten, remember!
Saying “If I forget you, Jerusalem” now, it You must remember!
וְתִפְקוֹד וְתִזְכּוֹר כְּאֵב צָר לַעֲכוֹר
פִּֽיהוּ לִסְכּוֹר לָנֽוּעַ כַּשִּׁכּוֹר
וְעוֹד בַּל־תִּמְכּוֹר נְקוּבֵי בֵּן בְּכוֹר
נְכוּרֵי בְלֶֽתֶךְ וָכוֹר וּפְאֵרָךְ אֶזְכּוֹר
וַאֲרַצְּךָ לִזְכּוֹר בְּפָרָשַׁת זָכוֹר׃
And account for the tormentor’s painful afflictions and remember;
Shut his mouth; let him stumble like one too drunk to remember.
And never again sell off those entitled the firstborn member,
Bought for a letekh and kor,[21] Find Hosea 3:2  as Your glory I remember.
And I will, as You wish, remember, with the portion of “Remember!”
חי וקיים
Ḥay v-Qayyam
חַי וְקַיָּם נוֹרָא וּמָרוֹם וְקָדוֹשׁ!
Living and enduring, awesome and exalted and holy!
אָץ קוֹצֵץ בֶּן קוֹצֵץ קְצוּצַי לְקַצֵּץ
בְּדִבּוּר מְפוֹצֵץ רְצוּצַי לְרַצֵּץ
לֵץ בְּבֹא לְלוֹצֵץ פֻּלַץ וְנִתְלוֹצֵץ
כְּעָץ מְחַצְּצִים לְחַצֵּץ כְּנֵץ עַל־צִפּוֹר לְנַצֵּץ׃
Expressly Thorn Thornson[22] ‘Amaleq  would trim off the tail,
with shattering speech, assailed ones to assail.
Laughing came the leerer, but was staggered, to wail:
“Fire arrows, like an eagle that swoops on a quail!”
עָב בָּקַע מֵרֹאשׁ יְחִידִים מֶֽנּוּ גְרוֹשׁ
חָל וַיֵּֽלֶךְ וַיִּפְרוֹשׁ וַיָּֽשׇׁב עַל־גַּבִּי חֲרוֹשׁ
זֻמַּן וְהׇנְקַב לְרֹאשׁ רֵאשִׁית גּוֹיִם לָרַע לִדְרוֹשׁ
הֱיוֹת לְכׇל־בּוֹגְדִים רֹאשׁ וְצִפְעוֹ תְּלוֹת בְּמִבְחַר בְּרוֹשׁ׃
As the cloud broke overhead to exclude some guys,[23] The tribe of Dan, which according to the midrash was the rearguard attacked by ‘Amaleq on account of not being protected by the clouds of glory, due to their tendency towards idol worship. 
he started off and attacked, with a back-plowing reprise.
Zenith he was entitled, first of nations to despise,
The arch-traitor whose viper[24] Haman  would to the choice cypress gallows rise.
רָץ וְהִקְרָה בַּדֶּֽרֶךְ עֲיֵפֵי טַרְחוּת דֶּֽרֶךְ
וּפָץ לֹא־זוֹ הַדֶּֽרֶךְ תָּעוּ בַמִּדְבָּר בִּישִׁימוֹן דָּֽרֶךְ
בְּנָחָשׁ עֲלֵי דֶֽרֶךְ שָׁלַח יָד בְּחַמּוּקֵי יֶֽרֶךְ
וְעַל כׇּל־רֹאשׁ דֶּֽרֶךְ זִנֵּב כׇּל־עוֹבְרֵי דָּֽרֶךְ׃
As he ran he came across the tired and weary on the way,
Saying, “Not this way,” they erred in the wild desert way.
Reaching at the thighs’ curve,[25] Israel. Find Song of Songs 7:2  at the snake on the way,[26] Dan. Find Genesis 49:17 
He cut down at each crossroad passers-by along the way.
יָעַף וְעָף וְחָשׁ וּמֵחוּר פֶּֽתֶן רָחַשׁ
וְנוֹדַע כִּי הוּחָשׁ מִשּֽׁוֹרֶשׁ נָחָשׁ
רָגַשׁ וְלָחַשׁ וְעֵת לֵדָתוֹ נִחַשׁ
וּבִכְשָׁפָיו הׇכְחַשׁ וּבְחַלָּשׁ נֶחֱלַשׁ וְלֹא־חָשׁ׃
He flew, flying quick, from the python crept from its pit,
Known from the snake’s root he would hoof it.

To us they fought in the wilderness.
But to You they made war in settlements.
To us outside the camp they harrassed;
But to You within the camp they roared.
To us the cloud’s exiles they violated;
But to You the clouded purgation-cover they razed.
To us they cut down the cumulus-ejected,
But to You ascending to the cumulus they sought.
To us outside the barrier they rose,
But to You within and without they went in.
To us far from the place[65] Jerusalem  they charged,
But to You within Your place they laid waste.
To us outside our dwellings they cast us down,
But to You in Your set abode they cut down.
To us the covenant-sign[66] Circumcision  they tore from us,
But to You ten covenantal nobilities[67] The Ten Commandments  they destroyed.
To us they rose as the humble’s[68] Moses  hands rested,
But to You they raised their hanging hand against You.
To us they plotted and conspired in secret
But to You Your secret mount-angels they vilely removed.

An acrostic of the five words in this verse — ק֤ׄוֹל ׀ רִׄנָּ֬ה וִׄישׁוּעָ֗ה בְּׄאׇהֳלֵ֥י צַׄדִּיקִ֑ים — are used as a backronym for the term קרובץ used in Ashkenazi practice to refer to the piyyuṭim added to the ‘amidah on Purim. This is a false etymology — it is actually just a derivation of קרובות. But it shows the links between this verse and Purim, despite the fact that this verse, as part of the Hallel service, is not actually said on Purim!
102 The Torah.
103 Israel
104 The angelic hosts
105 Job 41:7. Referring to the Leviathan.
106 The angelic hosts
107 Isaiah 26:2
108 Israel
109 From here the qedusha and the rest of the repetition of Shaḥarit is recited as usual. (Followers of the Vilna Gaon recite the qedushta after the repetition, so as not to interrupt it.)
